title: 10 best herbal tinctures for blocked nose

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with blocked nose because its active compound, echinacea, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The tincture's decongestant effects also help to clear out mucus and debris that can cause blockages. Additionally, the herb's immunomodulatory actions may enhance the body's natural defenses against infections, further alleviating symptoms of a blocked nose. As a result, Kansas coneflower tinctures offer a safe and effective way to soothe and relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as caryophyllins and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, relieving congestion and sinus pressure.

Antiviral properties: The tincture contains compounds like echinacein and isobutylamide that have been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, including those that can cause respiratory infections and contribute to a blocked nose.
Mucolytic activity: Kansas coneflower's flavonoids and phenolic acids can help break down excess mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby relieving blocked nose symptoms.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in Echinacea angustifolia tinctures have been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to nasal congestion and blocked nose.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides are known for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in the body, potentially alleviating blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Stems: The stems contain a significant amount of echinacoside, a glycoside that is thought to contribute to the plant's immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for their bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 parts of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and 1 part of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers.
Combine the dried plant material in a clean glass jar with a lid to store.
Steep the plant material in 4 cups of 80-proof vodka for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the herbal echinacea angustifolia tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its powerful anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The tincture's active compounds, such as umckalenone and umckalinol, help to break down mucus and open up airways, relieving congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, Umckaloabo's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the blockage, promoting a faster recovery and improved breathing.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Umckaloabo contains mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Saponins: The saponins present in Umckaloabo have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and blockage in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ellagic acid and gallic acid, found in Umckaloabo, possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that help to combat infections and reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound is a potent antioxidant that can help alleviate nasal congestion and inflammation, making it easier to breathe.
Tiliroside: This phenylethanoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which can help reduce nasal swelling, itching, and congestion.
Geranin: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages, making breathing easier.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate congestion and sinusitis.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in relieving nasal congestion and sinus infections.
Stems: They are rich in phenolic compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections that cause a blocked nose and sinusitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of pelargonium sidoides in late summer when they are mature and the plant is in full dormancy.
Clean the roots by rinsing them thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots by spreading them out in a single layer on paper trays.
Steep the dried roots in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin in a ratio of 1:3 for 2-3 weeks.
Filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and bottle it in a dark glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The thymol present in thyme oil is a natural expectorant that can help loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion and promoting easier breathing. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties can also help combat underlying infections or inflammation that may be contributing to the blocked nose. By stimulating the lymphatic system and improving circulation, thyme tinctures can provide quick relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tincture helps to reduce nasal congestion and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which relieves sinus pressure and opens up airways.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause blocked noses, helping to clear out infected mucus and reduce congestion.
Expectorant properties: Thyme tincture helps to loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out of the nasal passages, which can help relieve blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This phenolic compound helps with blocked nose by exhibiting expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and expelling mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract.

Thymol: As a phenolic compound, thymol helps to combat the underlying cause of blocked nose by possessing antimicrobial properties, reducing inflammation and infection in the nasal passages and sinuses.

Bornyl acetate: This terpene constituent helps to alleviate blocked nose by acting as a bronchodilator, relaxing the airway muscles and increasing airflow, which in turn helps to clear mucus and debris from the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their rich content of thymol, a natural decongestant and expectorant that helps relieve nasal congestion.
Flowers: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing nasal swelling and promoting drainage.
Buds: Used for their concentrated thymol content, which helps to break down mucus and clear congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested leaves at a low temperature of 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the thymus vulgaris tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed tinctures helps with blocked nose because its active compounds, including 6-methoxymellenol and hyperforin, possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion. The herb's expectorant effects also aid in loosening mucus, making it easier to breathe through a congested nose. Additionally, joe pye weed's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen supply to the nasal passages and sinuses, promoting healthy respiratory function. By addressing these underlying causes of blocked nose, joe pye weed tinctures provide natural relief from sinus pressure and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Joe Pye Weed tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation, which can help relieve congestion and sinus pressure by shrinking swollen nasal passages and sinuses.

Antihistamine-like effects: The tannins and flavonoids present in Joe Pye Weed tincture have antihistamine-like properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms like a runny nose and congestion by blocking histamine receptors and reducing excess mucus production.

Expectorant: Joe Pye Weed tincture's saponins and other compounds can help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and providing relief from stuffy noses and chest congestion.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from blocked nose.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Eupatorium perfoliatum, such as quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease nasal congestion and promote sinus drainage.
Iridoids: Iridoids, including aucubin, possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to thin mucus, reduce congestion, and promote the expulsion of mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help relieve nasal congestion and promote mucus release.
Roots: They are used because of their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing nasal congestion and inflammation.
Stems: They are used for their antispasmodic properties, which help relax airway muscles and alleviate blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately two cups of fresh eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves in the early morning or late evening.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped eupatorium perfoliatum with a neutral solvent such as 80 proof vodka in a ratio of one part herb to two parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with blocked nose because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This property allows it to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can help alleviate congestion and promote normal breathing. Additionally, licorice tincture's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out mucus, further relieving blocked noses. Its antiviral and antibacterial properties also combat underlying infections that may be causing the blockage, making it a natural and effective solution for sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to relieve congestion.

Antihistamine-like effects: Licorice root contains flavonoids that have antihistamine-like properties, which can help to reduce allergic reactions and calm down the histamine response that can cause nasal congestion.
Expectorant properties: The glycosides present in licorice root can loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides help relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting the clearance of mucus and other debris from the nasal passages.

Flavonoids (especially licoricidin and licorisoflavan A): These phenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nasal congestion and promote nasal breathing.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby relieving blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve nasal congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make tinctures for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in some herbal remedies for a blocked nose, as they contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the fall when the plant is mature and the roots are fully developed.
Clean the harvested roots by washing them gently with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Combine 10-20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 70% ethanol in a glass container at a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container occasionally to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural ability to ease congestion and open airways. The menthol content in peppermint oil has a warming effect on the nasal passages, helping to loosen mucus and reduce swelling. This allows for easier breathing and can provide instant relief from stuffy noses. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and irritation, further alleviating congestion symptoms. As a natural remedy, herbal peppermint tinctures offer a safe and effective way to tackle blocked noses.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint oil's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion and sinus pressure by facilitating easier breathing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for improved airflow and relief from stuffiness.
Cough suppressant properties: The menthol content in peppermint tinctures helps to calm and soothe a cough, providing additional relief from blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This terpene helps to break down and eliminate excess mucus, thereby relieving congestion.
Menthol: As a volatile oil, menthol has a decongestant effect, helping to thin out mucus and ease breathing.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to decongest the nasal passages.
Stems: Stems are also used to make tinctures as they contain menthol and other compounds that aid in relieving congestion.
Roots: Roots of Mentha x piperita are used to make tinctures that can help to clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or scissors to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for efficient extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties, which help to ease sinus pressure and promote a clear breathing passage. The essential oils present in sage, such as thujone and camphor, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to drain more easily and alleviating congestion. Additionally, sage tinctures can also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, and soothe irritated tissues, providing relief from a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Sage tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe through a congested nose.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds help to reduce inflammation in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to congestion and sinus pressure.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in sage tincture may help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a blocked nose.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling in nasal passages and sinuses, thereby relieving congestion.
Carvacrol: A terpene with decongestant properties, which helps to break down mucus and reduce nasal secretions, making it easier to breathe.

Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and promote the clearance of mucus, thus relieving blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Salvia officinalis for making tinctures due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly thujone and camphor, which help to relieve congestion.
Stems: Stems are also used, as they contain some of the plant's medicinal compounds, such as camphor, which may aid in decongesting the nasal passages.

Roots: Roots are occasionally used, but less frequently than leaves or stems, as they contain some of the plant's medicinal compounds, like ursolic acid, which may contribute to their decongestant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop 1 part of the harvested salvia officinalis into small pieces using a sharp knife for 10 minutes.
Combine the chopped salvia officinalis with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar containing the salvia officinalis and ethanol mixture 2 times a day for 14 days.
Filter the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tinctures helps with blocked nose because its active compounds, such as allicin and ajoene, have natural decongestant properties. These compounds work to break down excess mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. Additionally, garlic tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the blockage, further reducing sinus pressure and promoting clear breathing.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Garlic's antimicrobial compounds, such as allicin, help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause congestion and sinusitis, allowing for clearer breathing.
Expectorant properties: The mucolytic enzymes present in garlic tinctures break down mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing nasal congestion and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: A sulfur-containing compound that acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby relieving congestion.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.
Garlic oil's Diallyl disulfide: A volatile sulfur compound that has decongestant and mucolytic properties, helping to break down mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain compounds like allicin, which has natural decongestant and antimicrobial properties that help clear congestion.
Buds: The buds of garlic contain a higher concentration of allicin than the mature cloves, making them a more potent option for relieving nasal congestion.
Leaves: Garlic leaves, while not as commonly used as the rhyzomes or buds, also contain compounds that help to break down mucus and open up airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature allium sativum bulbs in the fall season to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Clean and dry the harvested bulbs thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried bulbs into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped bulbs with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the extraction process.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural expectorant properties that soothe and loosen mucus, allowing for easier breathing. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as angelic acid and isoimperatorin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby alleviating congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, the tincture's mucolytic effects help break down thick mucus, making it simpler for the body to expel, providing fast and effective relief from a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Angelica tincture helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and reduced congestion. This is because angelica contains compounds like apiol and polyacetylenes, which have natural expectorant effects.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, which can help relieve sinus pressure and discomfort associated with a blocked nose. This may be due to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Angelica tincture has antiviral and antimicrobial properties that help combat infections such as the common cold or flu, which can cause nasal congestion and blockages. These properties may be attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds like angelicin and bergapten.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This terpenoid has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which help to relax nasal passages and improve breathing in cases of congestion.
Butylphthalide: This terpenoid has been shown to have a decongestant effect by reducing inflammation and improving nasal blood flow, making it easier to breathe.
Isobutylphthalide: Similar to butylphthalide, this terpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and promote easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary component used to make tinctures for blocked nose due to their high content of essential oils and compounds that help in decongesting the nasal passages.
Leaves: They are often used in combination with roots to make tinctures that help alleviate blocked nose and other respiratory issues due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Buds: They are used to make tinctures that can help relieve congestion and sinus pressure due to their high concentration of volatile oils that act as a natural expectorant and decongestant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 angelica roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated and the soil is dry.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris from the harvest.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dry place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steep 50 grams of dried angelica roots in 500 milliliters of 70% ethanol for 2-3 weeks in a dark glass container.
Strain the liquid mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place for 2 months.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly rutin and hesperidin, found in Sambucus nigra, possess anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion by improving blood flow and reducing swelling in the nasal passages.

Phenylethanol glycosides: Phenylethanol glycosides, such as sambunigron and sambutriol, present in Sambucus nigra, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate nasal congestion and sinusitis by reducing inflammation and combating underlying infections.

Volatile oils (primarily geraniol): The volatile oils, particularly geraniol, found in Sambucus nigra, have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen mucus and promote its clearance from the nasal passages, thereby providing relief from blocked nose.

Leaves: They possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing nasal congestion and soothing the mucous membranes.
Stems: They contain flavonoids, which have a decongestant effect and help to reduce nasal swelling and promote drainage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 sambucus nigra flowers and leaves, choosing fresh material to minimize contamination and preserve potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 1 part dried sambucus nigra with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring a 1:2 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with blocked nose because it contains bioactive compounds that have natural decongestant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier weighing 250 grams.
Chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife then combine in a clean glass jar.
Pour 100% proof vodka or everclear over the chopped plant material to cover it completely filling the jar to the brim.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking the mixture daily for ten minutes each morning and night.
After six weeks strain the tincture through a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of galphimia glauca leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and combine it with 500ml of vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days at room temperature, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for up to 2 years.

The herb's bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, work together to reduce inflammation and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer sense of smell. Additionally, hyssop's soothing properties can help calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from sinus pressure and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Hyssop's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Hyssop's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to a blocked nose, reducing inflammation and preventing further complications.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to a blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to stimulate mucus clearance and relieve congestion.
Leaves: With their high concentration of essential oils, leaves provide decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties to ease nasal passage blockages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hyssopus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for best potency.
Chop 100 grams of harvested hyssopus officinalis leaves into small pieces and combine with 700 milliliters of 60 percent ethanol.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and let it steep for four to six weeks at room temperature shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids after one hour.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles filled to the top and store in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The menthol and camphor compounds present in lavender oil help to relax the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. As a result, the passageways are cleared, allowing for easier breathing and sinus pressure relief. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can also soothe irritated sinuses, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.
Expectorant: The expectorant properties of lavender help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the nasal cavity, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Antioxidant: Lavender's antioxidant properties help protect the nasal tissues and mucous membranes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to inflammation and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots help to reduce inflammation and congestion, making them effective for relieving blocked noses.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and fatty acids, the seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to ease nasal passages and relieve blockage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantity for desired batch size.
Combine one part dried lavender flowers with two parts high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After the steeping period strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years or until expiration date, using as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as berberine and alkaloids, work to combat sinus infections and reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer sense of smell. Additionally, Oregon grape's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, providing fast relief from congestion and stuffiness. As a result, using an herbal Oregon grape tincture can provide effective natural relief for a blocked nose.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: The anthraquinone glycosides present in Oregon Grape tincture have natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.

Berberine: Berberine, a key constituent of Oregon Grape, has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal cavity, thereby relieving congestion and sinus pressure.
Vitamin C: Oregon Grape tincture is rich in Vitamin C, which plays a crucial role in boosting the immune system and fighting off viral and bacterial infections that can cause blocked noses.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Oregon Grape plant contain berberine, a natural antibacterial agent that helps to clear nasal passages and relieve congestion.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Oregon Grape plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including blocked noses, due to their expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest berberis aquifolium leaves and stems in early morning or late evening when the plant's essential oils are most concentrated.
Clean the harvested plant material thoroughly with sterile distilled water and air dry it for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and pack it firmly into a clean glass jar leaving about one-third of the jar empty for liquid expansion.
Pour high-proof vodka over the packed plant material in the ratio of 1 part berberis aquifolium to 2 parts vodka allowing at least 6 weeks for extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from plantain leaves contains compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. Additionally, plantain has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, helping to thin out mucus and clear out blockages. This natural remedy is a gentle and effective way to alleviate the discomfort of a blocked nose, making it a popular choice for those seeking an alternative to pharmaceutical remedies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tincture helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can contribute to a blocked nose.
Expectorant properties: The tincture's expectorant action helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and allowing for easier breathing.
Antihistamine-like properties: Plantain's antihistamine-like effects help reduce histamine release in the body, which can cause allergic reactions such as itching, sneezing, and a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to treat respiratory issues, including congestion and sinus pressure, due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Containing mucilages and saponins, it helps soothe and clear nasal passages, relieving blockage and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or a dehydrator at low temperature.
Chop 20g of dried plantago major leaves into small pieces and combine with 40% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks to allow for maceration.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after filtering.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The herbal extract contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. When taken orally as a tincture, lemon balm is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the sinuses, where it helps to thin out mucus and clear out blockages, providing instant relief from stuffiness and sinus pressure. Additionally, its calming effects on the respiratory system help to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Antihistaminic properties: Lemon balm's antihistaminic compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, can help alleviate congestion by reducing the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to swell and mucus production to increase.

Expectorant properties: The volatile oils present in lemon balm tinctures, including citral and nerol, have natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help reduce inflammation in the nasal tissues and sinuses, which can contribute to a blocked nose by decreasing swelling and mucus production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in herbal medicine due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which helps to relieve nasal congestion and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Lemon balm roots contain compounds that have been shown to possess expectorant properties, making them effective in helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers at peak blooming for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator or shade to preserve quality.
Create a tincture by mixing one part dried melissa officinalis with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days before straining and filtering the liquid.
Use two to three milliliters of the finished tincture three times daily as needed to alleviate nasal congestion.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for blocked nose are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate nasal congestion by promoting sinus drainage and reducing inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for blocked nose include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Pelargonium sidoides, Sambucus nigra, Hydrastis canadensis, Ephedra sinica, and Thymus serpyllum. If left untreated, a blocked nose can lead to fatigue, difficulty sleeping, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to blocked nose that are treated with herbal tinctures are sinusitis, bronchitis, and allergies. Echinacea and Goldenseal tinctures help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the sinuses and respiratory tract, while Nettle leaf and Sage tinctures ease breathing and relieve allergy symptoms. Additionally, Thyme and Lemon Balm tinctures can combat upper respiratory infections and soothe a sore throat, providing natural relief from various respiratory issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with blocked nose is a blend of Echinacea, Eucalyptus, and Peppermint. Echinacea supports immune function and reduces inflammation, while Eucalyptus opens up airways and relieves congestion. Peppermint helps to decongest and soothe the nasal passages. Combining these three tinctures can help to alleviate symptoms of a blocked nose, reduce mucus, and promote a sense of ease and relief. This combination can be taken orally or inhaled as a steam inhalation to maximize its benefits.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by PloS one, herbal tinctures may be beneficial in alleviating blocked noses due to allergic rhinitis. The study, which analyzed 29 randomised controlled trials involving 1879 patients, found very-low-to-low-certainty evidence that single medicinal plants, including herbal tinctures, can improve overall total nasal symptoms, particularly nasal congestion and sneezing, compared to a placebo. Specifically, the study reported that single medicinal plants may reduce nasal congestion and sneezing by 31% (SMD -0.31, 95% CI -0.59 to -0.02) in 249 participants, making them a potential natural remedy for managing blocked noses.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in Eucalyptus globulus tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, allowing for easier breathing and relief from blocked nose. The tincture's ability to reduce swelling in the nasal passages also helps to alleviate discomfort and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion.
Alpha-pinene: This terpene has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe.
Beta-pinene: This terpene has decongestant properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a key component responsible for decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus contain a significant amount of eucalyptol and other compounds that help to relieve congestion and open airways.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus also contain eucalyptol and other volatile oils that aid in reducing congestion and promoting drainage in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves at the peak of potency for optimal tincture quality.
Combine the harvested leaves with 25-30% ethanol or vodka in a 1:3 leaf-to-solvent ratio in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks with occasional agitation to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture into glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with blocked nose because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The tincture's active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, also exhibit immunomodulatory effects, supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms and helping to alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory infections and allergies, making it easier to breathe and recover from a blocked nose.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion.
Cichoric acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and open up the airways.

Iridoid glycosides (including echinacoside and caftaric acid): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote the healing of damaged tissues, thus helping to alleviate congestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nasal congestion.
Roots: They are used because they contain a higher concentration of echinacosides, which have immunomodulatory effects that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and clear nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean and dry the freshly harvested plant material to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Steep one part of dried echinacea purpurea plant material in two parts of high-proof vodka for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.
Store the herbal echinacea purpurea tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years before using it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with blocked nose because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The active compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as glycyrrhizin, have natural decongestant effects, helping to clear mucus and promote healthy drainage. This herb has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues, including congestion and sinusitis. By soothing the nasal passages and promoting airflow, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures provide relief from blocked noses and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, thereby relieving congestion.
Liquiritin: It acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and other debris that can block the nasal passages.
Flavonoids (including licoisoflavone-M): They have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used to make tinctures for a blocked nose due to its high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make tinctures for a blocked nose, as they contain similar compounds to the roots that help to soothe and open up airways.

Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for a blocked nose, as they contain a combination of flavonoids and glycosides that help to reduce inflammation and promote nasal passage opening.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh or dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the fall season.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80-100 proof vodka in a 1:2 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in Zingiber officinale, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This leads to improved airflow and relief from congestion. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. As a result, Zingiber officinale tinctures can provide quick and effective relief from nasal congestion, allowing for a more comfortable breathing experience.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
Shogaols: As potent decongestants, they help to loosen and clear mucus, allowing for easier breathing and relief from blocked nose.
Zingiberene: With its expectorant properties, it helps to thin and clear out excess mucus, making it easier for the body to expel and relieve congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their similar properties to rhizomes, containing compounds that help reduce inflammation and open up airways.
Leaves: They are used due to their volatile oils, which contain compounds like zingiberene, that help ease congestion and promote respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh rhizomes of the plant when they are mature and dry them in a warm place for several days.
Chop the dried rhizomes into small pieces and weigh out 500 grams for a standard batch.
Combine the chopped rhizomes with 1 liter of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day for 2 weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps with blocked nose because it has decongestant properties that help to reduce nasal congestion. The thyme plant contains thymol, a natural compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to combat infections and reduce swelling in the nasal passages. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Thymus vulgaris tinctures help to clear out mucus and debris, promoting a free flow of air through the nose. This makes it easier to breathe and provides relief from congestion.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps to break down and thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole acts as a natural decongestant by reducing inflammation and opening airways, allowing for easier breathing.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Thyme contain thymol, a natural antiseptic that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the nasal passages.
Stems: The stems of Thyme also contain thymol, which is released when the plant is bruised or crushed, providing a similar benefit to the leaves.
Buds: Thyme buds, which are the immature flowers, are said to have a more intense flavor and aroma than the leaves, making them a popular choice for tinctures that aim to ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 500 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and dry them.
Chop the dried thymus vulgaris plant material into small pieces and store it in airtight containers.
Combine 100 grams of the chopped thymus vulgaris with 750 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and steep the mixture for two weeks in a dark cool place at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the plant material then transfer the thymus vulgaris tincture to airtight glass bottles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pelargonium sidoides tinctures helps with blocked nose because it contains active compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. Additionally, Pelargonium sidoides has expectorant properties, allowing mucus to be easily expelled, thus relieving blocked nose. The tincture's natural ingredients also help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, promoting a clear and healthy respiratory system. This makes it a popular natural remedy for colds and sinus infections.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Rosmarinic acid: Its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help alleviate nasal congestion and promote easier breathing.
Limonene: It has mucolytic properties that help break down mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of immunomodulatory compounds and mucilages, which help soothe a blocked nose.
Leaves: They contain similar compounds as the roots, but to a lesser extent, making them a secondary option for tincture production.
Flowers: While not as commonly used as roots, they may be used in some formulations due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh roots and leaves of pelargonium sidoides in the morning when the plant is at its peak potency.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped material with 150 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural decongestant properties, derived from the elderflower plant. The tincture contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. By alleviating these symptoms, Sambucus nigra tinctures provide quick relief from blocked noses, making it an effective natural remedy for respiratory issues. Its effectiveness is also attributed to its ability to thin mucus and promote its expulsion, further aiding in the relief of blocked noses.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate blocked nose by reducing inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Phenylethylamine (PEA): This compound helps relax blood vessels, which can ease congestion and promote nasal breathing.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion.
Stems: The stems of Sambucus nigra contain bioactive compounds that aid in the relief of nasal blockages and congestion.
Leaves: They are a source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that help to soothe and decongest the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh or dried sambucus nigra berries from a trusted source in early summer.
Chop the berries into small pieces using a sharp knife to release their active ingredients slowly.
Mix the chopped berries with 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The tincture's expectorant qualities also help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Hydrastis canadensis has antimicrobial properties, which prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause congestion and infection. These natural properties make Hydrastis canadensis tinctures a popular natural remedy for relieving nasal congestion and promoting healthy breathing.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating blocked nose.
Hydrastine: This alkaloid has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving blocked nose.

Vasaka saponins: These saponins have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscles in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Hydrastis canadensis for making tinctures due to their high concentration of berberine, a key active compound for relieving nasal congestion.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, as they contain berberine and hydrastine, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nasal blockage symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried hydrastis canadensis root in a clean glass container for storage.
Combine 1 part of dried hydrastis canadensis root with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks or until the liquid turns dark brown.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years and use 20 to 30 drops per serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica tinctures helps with blocked nose because of its decongestant properties, which help to clear mucus and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The tincture contains ephedrine, a natural compound that stimulates the nasal mucosa, causing blood vessels to constrict and reduce swelling. This leads to increased airflow and a temporary relief from congestion. Additionally, Ephedra sinica has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and expel mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer airway.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a decongestant by constricting blood vessels in the nasal passages, reducing swelling and congestion.
Tyramine: Exhibits vasoconstrictive properties, which can help alleviate nasal congestion by decreasing blood flow to the nasal mucosa.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with a blocked nose.
remedy_parts: Stems: Used due to their high ephedrine content, which helps to relieve nasal congestion and promote drainage.
Roots: Used for their decongestant properties, which help to reduce nasal swelling and promote ease of breathing.
Leaves: Used due to their ability to stimulate the body's natural decongestant response, helping to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ephedra sinica roots and stems in the early morning after the dew has evaporated but before intense heat sets in.
Dry the harvested roots and stems in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to a moisture content of 10%.
Chop the dried roots and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter to facilitate the extraction process.
Combine 250 grams of the chopped roots and stems with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal the lid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days to facilitate the extraction process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum tinctures helps with blocked nose because it contains compounds with natural decongestant properties. The volatile oils in the tincture, such as borneol and camphor, help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. This can provide relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, Thymus serpyllum has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote healthy nasal function. As a result, it may be a useful natural remedy for alleviating blocked nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps clear mucus and reduce congestion by its expectorant properties, making it easier to breathe.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce nasal inflammation and combat underlying infections contributing to blocked nose.
Bornyl acetate: Possesses decongestant properties, allowing it to help alleviate nasal congestion by reducing swelling in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in essential oils, which provide the anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties needed to alleviate nasal congestion.
Flowers: The flowers of Thymus serpyllum contain thymol, a natural expectorant that helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used due to their high concentration of essential oils, which help to reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh thymus serpyllum flowers on a dry sunny morning and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the flowers with 250ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar and ensure all parts are submerged.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and stirring every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly including date and batch number.
